report_name: UAC-0247 Espionage Campaign Targets Ukrainian Government and Healthcare with Data-Stealing Malware

threat_actors.name: UAC-0247
threat_actors.description: A threat actor group conducting a cyber-espionage campaign against Ukrainian government and healthcare sectors since March 2026, using phishing and a multi-stage infection chain to deploy data-stealing malware.
threat_actors.goals: Gathering intelligence on Ukrainian government operations and the state of civilian services during a time of conflict.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack is initiated via a malicious link in a phishing email, often themed around humanitarian aid.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The victim is tricked into opening a malicious .LNK shortcut file contained within a downloaded ZIP archive.

attack_patterns.name: System Binary Proxy Execution: Mshta
attack_patterns.description: The LNK file launches an HTA file, which is executed by mshta.exe to run scripts that can download and execute further payloads.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The infection chain involves the use of scripting, with PowerShell being a likely interpreter used within the HTA file or subsequent stages.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: The HTA script creates a scheduled task to launch the main executable payload, establishing persistence.

attack_patterns.name: Process Injection
attack_patterns.description: The malware injects its malicious shellcode into the legitimate Windows process RuntimeBroker.exe to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The malware payload steals data from the local system, specifically targeting files related to web browsers and the WhatsApp desktop client.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials from Password Stores
attack_patterns.description: The malware specifically targets and steals credential files, cookies, and history from Chromium-based browsers.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The stolen data is exfiltrated back to an attacker-controlled command-and-control server.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploited XSS vulnerabilities on legitimate sites to host malicious files, which victims downloaded after clicking a phishing link, constituting a form of drive-by compromise.

malware.name: RAVENSHELL-like backdoor
malware.description: A data-stealing payload injected into RuntimeBroker.exe, designed to exfiltrate data from Chromium-based browsers and the WhatsApp desktop application. It functions as a reverse TCP shell.

malware.name: RAVENSHELL
malware.description: A known backdoor which the UAC-0247 payload resembles in functionality.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
vulnerabilities.description: Attackers exploited Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities on legitimate websites to host their malicious ZIP archives for download.

identities.name: Ukrainian Government Organizations
identities.description: A primary target sector for the UAC-0247 espionage campaign, including various government bodies in Ukraine.

identities.name: Ukrainian Healthcare Facilities
identities.description: A primary target sector for the UAC-0247 espionage campaign, including municipal clinics and hospitals in Ukraine.

identities.name: CERT-UA
identities.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine, which issued the alert about the UAC-0247 campaign.

infrastructure.name: UAC-0247 Payload Hosting Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: AI-generated websites or legitimate sites with XSS vulnerabilities used by UAC-0247 to host malicious ZIP archives for download.

infrastructure.name: UAC-0247 C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled server used to receive data exfiltrated by the RAVENSHELL-like backdoor.

indicators.name: Suspicious HTA File Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious HTA File Execution by Email Client or Browser
id: f1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects mshta.exe being launched by a common email client or web browser, which is a TTP used by UAC-0247.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161942/apt/uac-0247-expands-cyber-campaign-across-ukraine.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of an HTA file via mshta.exe, a key step in the UAC-0247 infection chain.

indicators.name: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation by Script Interpreter
id: a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task by a script interpreter like mshta.exe, a persistence technique used by UAC-0247.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161942/apt/uac-0247-expands-cyber-campaign-across-ukraine.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.persistence
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\schtasks.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/create'
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a scheduled task, which UAC-0247 uses for persistence.

indicators.name: Anomalous RuntimeBroker Network Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous RuntimeBroker Network Activity
id: b3c4d5e6-f7a8-4c9d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6f7a
status: experimental
description: Detects RuntimeBroker.exe initiating outbound network connections, which is atypical behavior and could indicate process injection as used by UAC-0247.
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/161942/apt/uac-0247-expands-cyber-campaign-across-ukraine.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1055
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\RuntimeBroker.exe'
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter:
    DestinationIp|cidr:
      - '127.0.0.0/8'
      - '::1/128'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects RuntimeBroker.exe making outbound network connections, which is atypical and could indicate process injection as used by UAC-0247.
